target.name: Breaker Industries
target.founder: Matthew Buffa, Michael Irwin, Vanja Videnovic

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Breaker Industries is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Matthew Buffa, Michael Irwin, and Vanja Videnovic, having raised $6M to date. The Defense & Security sector comp universe comprises 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including established players like Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI.

Valuation analysis employed stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using the $6M total raised as a base value proxy and applying a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier yields an implied valuation of $15M. Peer-median analysis was not executable; while 19 same-sector comparables exist, none of the filtered subsets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) contained the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability, and a broad sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given Breaker's specific positioning.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M (low) to $17M (high), centered on $15M. This reflects standard early-stage progression mechanics but carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include the reliance on a single method and the absence of comparable Seed-stage Defense & Security transactions in the available dataset. Investors should treat this range as illustrative rather than definitive, pending additional comparable data or near-term milestone achievement.
